Home / Break-Even Calculator

Break-Even Calculator

Calculate how many months until you break even on your initial investment. Model different cost and revenue scenarios.

How to Use This Tool

1
Enter your total monthly costs (hosting, tools, freelancers, etc.).
2
Enter your average monthly revenue.
3
Enter your total initial investment before launch.
4
Review how many months until you break even.
5
Scroll down to see 9 comparison scenarios with different cost/revenue/investment combos.
6
tools.solopreneur-break-even-calculator.how_to_use.5

Frequently Asked Questions

What counts as an initial investment?

Any upfront costs before you start earning: development costs, design, legal fees, initial marketing spend, equipment purchases, and money spent during the build phase.

What is a good break-even timeline for a solopreneur?

3-12 months is typical for digital products and SaaS. If it takes more than 18 months, consider simplifying your MVP or finding earlier revenue sources.

Should I include my living expenses in monthly costs?

Yes, if this is your full-time venture. Include all personal expenses you need to cover. This gives you the real break-even point.

What if my monthly revenue fluctuates?

Use your average over the last 3 months. If you are pre-revenue, use conservative estimates — it is better to be pleasantly surprised than caught short.

How can I speed up my break-even?

Launch an MVP sooner, offer pre-sales, add a service layer (consulting), or reduce scope. Time to market is the biggest lever for solopreneurs.

Results

Copy All
#1 📊 Break-Even Analysis • Initial Investment: $5,000 • Monthly Costs: $500/mo • Monthly Revenue: $1,000/mo • Net Monthly Profit: $500/mo ⏱️ Months to Break Even: 10 months (0.8 years) 📈 Within reach. 10 months to break even. Focus on boosting revenue to speed this up. 💡 Tip: To break even faster, either increase monthly revenue (raise prices, upsell, add revenue streams) or reduce monthly costs (automate, outsource cheaper, cut unnecessary tools).
#2 Scenario 1: $50/mo costs + $100/mo revenue + $500 invested → 10 months to break even
#3 Scenario 2: $100/mo costs + $250/mo revenue + $1,000 invested → 7 months to break even
#4 Scenario 3: $500/mo costs + $1,000/mo revenue + $5,000 invested → 10 months to break even
#5 Scenario 4: $1,000/mo costs + $2,000/mo revenue + $10,000 invested → 10 months to break even
